markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Onboard Kalil to pipeline operations and advanced customization.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=20.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Automate Post-Processing:** Convert manual Claude edits into automated pipeline steps to improve efficiency and consistency.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
  - [**Use Claude for Customization:** Use Claude to add new steps (e.g., FAQ) by feeding it your pipeline YAML and a working example from another client.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=628.0)
  - [**Prioritize Artifacts for Tuning:** Store instructions in separate artifacts (700–1k tokens) for easier, non-technical tuning, as editing them is simpler than modifying the pipeline YAML directly.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=760.0)
  - [**Debug with Flow:** Use the Flow monitor to trace agent decisions and identify bottlenecks, like the fact-checker removing links, which led to a process fix.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)

## Topics

### Pipeline Customization & Automation

  - [**Goal:** Automate manual post-processing steps (currently done in Claude) by converting them into pipeline steps.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
  - [**Method:** Use Claude to generate new steps.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=628.0)
      - [**Input:** Your pipeline's YAML code + a working example from another client (e.g., Biologica's Editorial V2 pipeline).](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=628.0)
      - [**Prompt:** Ask Claude to integrate the example step into your pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=695.0)
  - [**Instruction Storage:**](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=760.0)
      - [**Artifacts (Recommended):** Store instructions in separate files referenced by the pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=760.0)
          - [**Why:** Easier to edit and tune without touching the pipeline's code.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=848.0)
      - [**Inline:** Embed instructions directly in the pipeline YAML.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=788.0)
          - [**Why:** Requires editing the YAML itself, which is less efficient for frequent adjustments.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=788.0)
  - [**Artifact Token Limit:**](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=980.0)
      - [**Guideline:** Aim for 700–1,000 tokens per artifact.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=980.0)
      - [**Why:** Large artifacts can degrade agent performance and cause instructions to be missed.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=980.0)

### Debugging & Optimization with Flow

  - [**Tool:** The Flow monitor provides a detailed log of every agent decision.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=1293.0)
  - [**Use Cases:**](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=1293.0)
      - [**Identify Bottlenecks:** Trace agent reasoning to find where a step is failing or taking too long.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=1568.0)
      - [**Fix Process Flaws:** The Monograph pipeline's fact-checker was removing links.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
          - [**Problem:** The fact-checker ran *after* the linker.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
          - [**Solution:** Move the fact-checker step to run *before* the linker.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)

### Pipeline Ramp-Up Strategy

  - [**Initial Focus:** Operate existing pipelines and perform manual post-processing in Claude.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=499.0)
  - [**Next Goal:** Automate repetitive manual corrections by converting them into pipeline steps.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=1740.0)
  - [**Learning Path:**](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=1889.0)
    1.  [**Observe:** Review complex pipelines (e.g., Biologica Editorial V2) to see advanced capabilities.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=187.0)
    2.  [**Iterate:** Add one new step at a time to your existing pipeline to learn the process.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=1126.0)
  - [**Timeline:** A pipeline typically reaches a stable state after 2–3 batches (2–3 weeks), with initial batches requiring more manual work.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=1647.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Kalil:**](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=1830.0)
      - [Explore the Biologica Editorial V2 pipeline in Atlas to see advanced post-processing steps.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=187.0)
      - [Use Claude to add one new step (e.g., FAQ, TLDR) to an existing pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=592.0)
      - [Use the Flow monitor to trace agent decisions and understand pipeline execution.](https://fathom.video/share/98ahBCQVhhSHUZwCRwqwNqvrZ2GWDscn?tab=summary&timestamp=1293.0)
